checks.finding: design.md Architecture and State model justify the authoritative registry as preventing implicit legacy signals, paired adapters as preserving portable enforcement, and review-contract reuse as common identity/shape validation; Compatibility and versioning explains the minor release as backward-compatible enforcement and metadata.
design.md contains no Open Questions entries; no unresolved owner, blocking classification, or resolution path is present.
design.md contains no Assumptions entries, and its repository-state claims are grounded by investigation.md INV-001 through INV-008.

design.md addresses the applicable requirements.md Constraints through a versioned registry schema, bounded pre-v1.3.0 legacy declarations, continued quality-gate requirements for Done, and synchronized plugin/marketplace versioning; no direct constraint contradiction was found. ADR existence is owned by impl-reviewer-a and is not duplicated here.
No performance requirements found in requirements.md.

design.md has no Deployment / CI Plan section. Its Integration section names workflow jobs but does not identify the deployment/release target or explicitly state whether rollout needs a feature flag and whether CI needs environment variables or secrets. This leaves release and CI rollout implementation ambiguous; the check contract classifies an absent plan as blocking Major.

design.md Architecture and Migration add repository artifacts and historical records but declare no database table or column changes; therefore no database migration path, rollback, or >10k-row impact plan is required.

design.md references only repository-internal scripts, workflow entry points, and the existing review-contract foundation; it introduces no external system, third-party service, or cross-service API call requiring an integration contract and outage behavior.

design.md Architecture, State model, Integration, Migration, Failure behavior, and Compatibility and versioning cover all four requirements.md Goals: persistent-state enforcement, preservation of existing gates, bounded historical migration, and POSIX/PowerShell parity. No unrelated component, data model, or endpoint is introduced.

design.md Security considerations makes high-risk claims that path traversal and symlink escapes fail, malformed artifacts fail closed, and spec/registry content is never executed, but design.md names no command, CI job, acceptance-test artifact, metric, or review artifact that validates those security claims. Without a concrete design-level validation path, implementation and the downstream gate cannot determine the required security evidence; this is blocking Major under the check contract.
